author: y4rr
content: Fourth test passed. Framework now mature. Subagent followed all rules without prompting: canonical grep shape used verbatim every time (with their own extension to also filter \.claude/ worktrees), workspace build run for all 4 pub API changes (Profiler Debug, ProfileReport Debug+Clone, PendingCudaScope visibility narrowing, lint config), severity reassessment used 3 times productively (eprintln warn_poisoned 🔴→🟡 removal-not-replacement; panic in with_profiler 🔴→🟢 unreachable invariant; current_thread_id audit-was-wrong). Two notable wins: (1) the audit's 🟢 fix recommendation (use ThreadId::as_u64()) was actually wrong — that API is still unstable; subagent attempted the fix, hit E0658, reverted, kept format-parse with explanatory comment. This is exactly the audit-overstatement-detection the new severity-reassessment step was designed to catch. (2) eprintln-removal clause was applied with subtle judgment: workspace lacks tracing/log; warn_poisoned message has no Result return path; subagent considered surfacing via Result (would change ~20 internal call sites) and chose removal-with-no-op-method-and-explanatory-doc — defensible per the clause's third bullet. Subagent suggested 1 new refinement: stdlib stability verification clause in §1 (check rustc version, attempt minimal probe before trusting docs.rs stability claims). Otherwise framework appears converged. 11 fixes applied, 1 deferred (ProfileSchedule Clone semantics needs callback ownership decision).
